other_observers: John Stulp, Janeal Thompson, Dotti Russell, Duane Nelson, Brandon Percival, Lisa Edwards, Cole Wild;
specific_location: 24247 Hwy 287 (5 miles south of the south edge of Lamar on Hwy 287 and 1/4 mile to the west in the backyard of the farmhouse)
description: Bright green crown and back, between 3 1/2 and 4 inches in length, white patch behind eye with grayish patch below contrasting with chin, a single red spot on gorget, light breast, buffy green sides and flanks, green tail (black bar on each side with 3 rounded white tips - notch in center)Behaviors: The bird had difficulty in feeding in the hard wind and pumped its tail some. It darted in and out about every ten minutes and disappeared to the north of the house for a windbreak. On the 6th the wind let up, the bird fed more leisurely and did not pump its tail.Call: not notedPlumage: immature
photographer: Photographer: Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Janeal Thompson
